---
title: "Predict bounded continuous distributions"
author: "David J. H. Shih"
pdf-engine: pdflatex
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      - file: math.sty
---

# Continuous distribution in [0, 1]

Suppose a neural network generates a real vector $\vec{a} \in
\mathcal{R}^J$.

We use $\vec{a}$ to parameterize a mixture of $K = J / 3$ beta distributions such that
$$
\begin{aligned}
\vec{\pi} = \text{softmax}(\vec{a}_{1:K}) \\
\vec{\alpha} = (\exp a_k)_{k=K+1}^{2K} \\
\vec{\beta} = (\exp a_k)_{k=2K+1}^{3K} \\
\vec{\theta} = ( \vec{\pi}, \vec{\alpha}, \vec{\beta} ) .
\end{aligned}
$$

The cumulative distribution is
$$
F( x \mid \vec{\theta} ) =
 \sum_k  \pi_k F_{\text{Beta}}( x \mid \alpha_k, \beta_k ) ,
$$
where $F_{\text{Beta}}$ is the cumulative distribution function of the beta
distribution.

# Continuous distribution in [a, b]

Use $F(x \mid \alpha_k, \beta_k) =  F_{\text{Beta}}( \frac{x - a}{b - a} \mid \alpha_k, \beta_k )$
as the base distribution in the mixture.


# Other distributions

Use $\vec{a}$ to parameterize a mixture of distributions with appropriate
transformations on each $a_j$, involving

- softmax for mixture weights
- exp for a non-negative parameter
- logistic for a parameter in [0, 1]
